title: Triple integration of Imperial IDE Workbench, WARP Zone, and MetaOS orchestration on PC
mission_summary: Use the three bundled candidate source archives as the foundation for a careful, working integration: Imperial IDE Workbench, WARP hot development zone, and MetaOS orchestration. Compile them into Imperium New Reality through the current PC root, wire them to Mechanicus, Astronomicon, Administratum, and the Imperial IDE control shell, keep unsafe execution blocked, validate, commit, and push.

strategic_intent.kernel: Imperium New Reality is the kernel.
strategic_intent.workbench: Workbench becomes the first owner-facing visual and TUI control surface.
strategic_intent.warp: WARP becomes the hot development workroom inside the system.
strategic_intent.metaos: MetaOS becomes the orchestration brain for routing, thin servitors, organ chronicles, and bundle release gates.
strategic_intent.mechanicus: Mechanicus remains the tool guardian, validator, bridge authority, dry-run gateway, and receipt owner.
strategic_intent.operator_goal: The Owner should begin using the system from inside the system instead of manually moving files between surfaces.

organ_route.DOCTRINARIUM: candidate admission, canon boundaries, WARP and MetaOS doctrine, no overclaim
organ_route.OFFICIO_AGENTIS: owner-facing Russian launch guide and final summary
organ_route.ASTRONOMICON: task entry, task dashboard, current task, registered task and launch card visibility
organ_route.ADMINISTRATUM: source bundle provenance, import receipts, chronicles, bundle gate, continuity, push closure
organ_route.MECHANICUS: bridge adapters, tool registry, command policy, dry-run gateway, validation receipts
organ_route.INQUISITION: unsafe execution block, fake-green prevention, candidate promotion downgrade, smoke gates
organ_route.STRATEGIUM: large-step integration sequence, usage ladder, next real execution gates
organ_route.SCHOLA_IMPERIALIS: operator workflow lessons and reusable integration patterns

bundled_binary_archives.note: Bundled ZIPs are candidate payload archives. Their internal language is handled during integration and not treated as taskpack root text.

cyrillic_in_taskpack_root_files.note: Compatibility field. The canonical admission key is language_and_encoding_policy.cyrillic_in_taskpack.

localization_exception.scope: owner-facing runtime files only after Officio role entry

cyrillic_in_taskpack.scope: MANIFEST.json, TASK_SPEC.md, ACCEPTANCE_GATES.md, OUTPUT_REQUIREMENTS.md, TASK_ROUTE_MANIFEST_TEMPLATE.json, TASK_START_ACK_TEMPLATE.json

binary_bundles_exception.reason: Source bundles are binary candidate payloads and are inventoried during execution, not parsed as taskpack root policy text.

allowed_when: bundled source archive hashes and inventories are recorded
imports are under allowed destination paths only
Workbench smoke passes or exact blocker is recorded
WARP smoke passes or exact blocker is recorded
MetaOS smoke passes or exact blocker is recorded
Mechanicus bridges remain dry-run by default
real execution, unrestricted shell, live LLM backend, and persistent daemon remain blocked
no secret files or local operator configs are staged
forbidden_push_cases: failed validation without explicit BLOCK verdict
secrets, credentials, or local operator configs staged
destructive cleanup or delete without owner batch approval
live LLM backend enabled with secrets
candidate bundle imported as final full IDE without smoke receipts

primary_outputs: Bundled source archive provenance and inventories
Imperial IDE shell commands and panels for workbench, warp, and metaos
Mechanicus bridge adapters for workbench, warp, and metaos
Administratum bundle gate wiring for WARP and MetaOS
Thin servitor runtime and organ chronicle integration as dry-run or candidate
